tools.title: American Epidemic Timeline
tools.summary: A carefully cited timeline of major U.S.-linked epidemics, outbreaks, and public-health turning points.
tools.evidence_model: Cited entries with confidence labels, archival visuals, uncertainty notes, and careful public-health interpretation
tools.long_note: The timeline follows major epidemics, public-health turning points, and selected regional outbreaks while keeping uncertain diagnoses and rough counts visibly uncertain.

tools.title: Pathogen Atlas
tools.summary: A cited digital exhibit about pathogen origins, reservoirs, transmission ecology, historical spread, and evidentiary uncertainty.
tools.evidence_model: Origin confidence, evidence-type panels, citations, and uncertainty notes
tools.long_note: The pathogen atlas runs as a dark digital exhibit with confidence labels, evidence-type panels, citations, and explicit uncertainty notes.

tools.title: Maritime Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A cited map-first digital exhibit on ships, ports, quarantine, naval warfare, captivity, trade, migration, and maritime disease ecology.
tools.evidence_model: Case modules with citations, route confidence, burden notes, uncertainty notes, mechanism filters, and guided-tour narrative
tools.long_note: This is the main public-history exhibit in the atlas family: an exploratory disease ecology surface with case modules, route confidence labels, and guided-tour and presentation modes for later video adaptation.

tools.title: Revolutionary War Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A historical epidemiology section focused on colonial and Revolutionary-era disease ecology, troop movement, and the public-health constraints around the founding period.
tools.long_note: A structured historical atlas page connecting colonial disease ecology, military movement, smallpox, inoculation, and public-health constraints around the founding period.

tools.title: Viking Health Atlas
tools.summary: A Norse-world disease and health map that ties Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, and pre-Columbian contact arguments into one visual surface.
tools.evidence_model: Map-based historical synthesis plus linked essays
tools.long_note: A Norse-world health and disease map connecting Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, pre-Columbian contact arguments, and epidemic uncertainty.
